mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce equity discount rates and lower corporate borrowing costs, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and shrinking consumer spending, which forces equity markets to price in lower corporate earnings.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly declines in retail sales and housing starts confirm a broad collapse in consumer demand, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal yields positive 20-day returns in over 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production trigger short-term market panic and negative equity returns, but this mechanism only activates when inflation remains persistently elevated.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation produce positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising interest rates combined with falling unemployment create a hard landing risk that compresses equity valuations and drives negative forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields positive 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below one percent signals recessionary conditions, causing investors to price in severe earnings downgrades and negative forward equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth falls below 1.0 percent
failure_condition: GDP growth below one percent is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll additions reflect robust economic activity and corporate revenue growth, driving positive short-term equity momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices alongside falling retail sales indicate stagflationary pressure, forcing institutional investors to reduce equity allocations and driving negative returns.
failure_condition: Stagflation signals produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases.
